query: Compare Adidas presence in third-party event coverage (World Cup, Olympics, Euros) vs in its own brand films (NMD, YEEZY, Samba, You Got This).

payload.entity: Adidas visual presence across third-party event coverage vs owned brand films
payload.query: Compare Adidas presence in third-party event coverage (World Cup, Olympics, Euros) vs in its own brand films (NMD, YEEZY, Samba, You Got This).

timeline.dominant_theme: EARNED: World Cup kits + sideline boards
EARNED: Fevernova match ball + tournament highlights

representative_clip.title: Iconic World Cup moments compilation featuring Adidas Fevernova

timeline.dominant_theme: EARNED: Jabulani match ball + South Africa tournament branding

representative_clip.title: 2010 FIFA World Cup South Africa coverage with Jabulani and boards
Adidas Team GB / Paralympics GB kit reveal
2014 FIFA World Cup Final Germany vs Argentina

timeline.dominant_theme: EARNED: Rio athlete uniforms vs OWNED: Parley collaboration

representative_clip.title: Adidas Originals x Parley collaboration film

timeline.dominant_theme: EARNED: Telstar 18 ball + national team kits
EARNED: Al Rihla match ball + Argentina/France kits
EARNED: Euro 2024 sideline boards + Spain kit

payload.narrative_summary:
Adidas shows up more consistently in earned media than in owned media across this corpus. The earned pattern is especially strong in football and major multisport events: FIFA World Cup footage repeatedly surfaces Adidas through official match balls, national team kits, and perimeter boards, while Olympic clips show the brand on athlete uniforms and footwear. This makes Adidas feel structurally embedded in elite sport, with visibility tied to competition, broadcast spectacle, and tournament iconography rather than explicit promotion.

Owned presence is more intermittent but more controlled and product-led. When Adidas appears in its own films, the visuals are tighter and more intentional: campaign montages, Team GB kit unveilings, Parley collaboration imagery, and product storytelling such as Yeezy. In other words, earned exposure supplies scale and continuity, while owned exposure supplies narrative precision and style. In this dataset, earned footage dominates the year-by-year cadence, while owned content spikes around launches, collaborations, and campaign moments.

estimated_value.calculation_basis: Conservative clip-based estimate using only visually verifiable Adidas presence in the available corpus. Counted distinct clips where Adidas appears via kits, match balls, sideline boards, trefoil/three-stripes, or product hero shots. Omitted clips with unclear year attribution even if Adidas was likely present. Estimated brand-intelligence value assumes roughly $2,500 per validated visual-presence clip for comparative monitoring utility across earned and owned media.
